fixes_applied.description: Physical clamp: P_bowmans >= Pc_pt + P_interstitial = 19 mmHg. Prevents spurious SNGFR=56.4 attractor from tubular compliance formula calibrated for human length scales.
nom_cd_na_reabsorption computed including SGLT2-coupled Na reabsorption at normal BG. Value: 0.5680 (vs human M003: 0.634).

blood_glucose_mg_dl('normal') changed from 120 to 90 mg/dL = BG_nom. Ensures BG_excess=0 for C57BL/6J lean control mice, stopping delta_Daa evolution that caused long-term (t=200000 min) SNGFR creep and Na balance collapse.

root_cause_of_long_term_divergence: BG_normal=120 > BG_nom=90 produced BG_excess=30 mg/dL, driving d(delta_Daa)/dt=1.875e-6/min. Over 200,000 min, delta_Daa grew to ~0.19 (19% afferent dilation), increasing SNGFR from 31.8 to ~64 nL/min. This caused massive Na_excretion > Na_intake, charging the VP integral, and ultimately MAP -> -691,138 mmHg at t=200,000.
notes: run_to_ss completed in 0.7 s on cemrg001 (kidney env, LSODA rtol=1e-5/atol=1e-7)
Na_conc = 140.000 mmol/L at SS (VP integral fully equilibrated, no drift)
dsodium at SS = -2.66e-10 mEq/min (numerical noise only, not physiology)
Oncotic pressure equilibrates from 10.17 (initial) to 10.33 mmHg (self-consistent with ppc=3.4, SNRBF=180 nL/min, FF=0.177)
